# Wiretap 0.1.0

The first public release of Wiretap: a local-first macOS menu bar recorder for system audio and your default microphone.

## Highlights

- Capture system audio and microphone together in one AAC `.m4a` file.
- Suppress speaker leakage while keeping system audio clean.
- Continue recording through default input and output device changes.
- Browse, search, rename, play, reveal, export, share, and delete recordings locally.
- Play recordings at 1×, 1.1×, 1.24×, 1.5×, or 2× speed.
- Recover source files after interruptions or failed finalization.
- See an explicit saving state while long recordings are processed.

## Requirements

- macOS 15 or later.
- Microphone permission.
- Screen & System Audio Recording permission. macOS may request an app relaunch after this permission is first granted.

## Install

1. Download `Wiretap-0.1.0.dmg` below.
2. Open the DMG and drag Wiretap into Applications.
3. Launch Wiretap and complete the permission setup.
4. Start recording from the menu bar or press `Command-Shift-R`.

The DMG is signed with Developer ID and notarized by Apple. A SHA-256 checksum is provided alongside the download.

Wiretap stores recordings locally under its Application Support directory. It has no accounts, cloud sync, analytics, or third-party runtime dependencies.
